raw_consumer_statement:
XXXX ( and XXXX, and XXXX, same company that uses different names ) is a XXXX company running scam ads on XXXX. I have complained to XXXX and they make all kinds of promises, but the ads are still there. This company goes by multiple different names. I placed another order with XXXX  and this is how I found out it is the same company. 

Anyway, on XX/XX/XXXX I placed an order with XXXX for {$35.00}. On XX/XX/XXXX, I placed a small order for {$29.00} with XXXX. I received marketing emails trying to get me to purchase more. At the time, they operated on these websites : XXXX XXXX XXXX, which I accessed through ads found on XXXX. I never received either order I placed so I sent an email asking about my orders ( same company so one email ). They claimed I received it. I didn't. They sent a tracking number but when I checked the tracking, there was no identifying information on it proving it was my package. Then they sent me a snip of a confirmation delivery which had no identifying information, like my name or address. I called them out and said this was a bogus receipt as it did not have my information on it. They kept making promises so I called my credit card company, Citibank Mastercard. 

At first, they refunded me both transactions. Then they demanded proof, so I sent what I had. They denied my claim, even though there is no proof of delivery, so I am XXXX. I sent Citi snips from all the websites that have given this vendor a scam rating/warning, but Citi certainly isn't going to let that be a factor. I have never received anything for either order and Citi refuses to protect my fraudulent purchases, even though they promise that they do. How can they deny my claims if there is no legitimate proof of delivery?
